title: adenostemma madurense
intro: Adenostemma madurense, also known as the Philippine false dragonhead or madureño, is a plant species native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce fever and relieve pain due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Adenostemma madurense include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of fever. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are often combined with other medicinal plants to enhance their therapeutic effects.
